item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This article has an amazing concept and I love the details, but the grammar/spelling/word use seemed really unprofessional and jolted me a little every time I came across an instance. It made the article unenjoyable to read, so I feel the need to downvote.

item.title: Re: Operational Staff Post
item.content:encoded:
Added, thank you.

Article has also been locked just in case.

item.title: Re: Operational Staff Post
item.content:encoded:
You can edit a comment into the source, that says that this is correct, just like with mnestics.

  in-situ[!--"IN-SITU” IS CORRECT, NOT "ON-SITE", DO NOT FIX--]

item.title: Operational Staff Post
item.content:encoded:
People, stop changing the spelling. It's in-situ, not in-site.

In-situ is a legitimate term, meaning "in the original place."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
In my opinion the best part is the fact this ghost driver is a normal guy (aside from being a ghost) who doesn't work on certain days, and that he is very secretive and does not like to be seen by people, probably due to being a ghost…
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really like it! Only one thing. It's "ordnance," not "ordinance" for unexploded military material. The latter is like a decree of a Church

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A quick nitpick here. Where you censored the line names, 2 of them don't actually correspond to any of the current lines. There is no line with 5 letters in it, and there is no line with 9 letters in it.
You may be better off using blocks of 7, 8 and 8. This way, it keeps some ambiguity in what the lines are.
███████, for example, could refer to either the Jubilee Line or the Central Line (Grey and Red on the tube map, respectively)
████████, on the other hand, can refer to the Bakerloo (Brown), District (Green), Northern (Black) or Victoria (Light Blue) lines.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
38 Phase-2 events in the past decade, only one of which required the use of the fail-safe explosive charges.

Goddamn. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This one's great! I love how much personality is revealed by the details of when this effect doesn't work. And I assume the derailing action is just a matter of this entity getting sorely peeved by someone suggesting it's not actually there. I mean, that kind of thing can really hurt, y'know?
Hey hey +100 yay

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The image in this article was removed because we could not properly determine whether or not the image was properly licensed or attributed. The image is a copyrighted stock photo from shutterstock. Please contact anqxyr or another member of the Image Team if you have questions.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Only thing I don't like is the censored train lines, if you live in London, its really easy to work out
████████, █████████ and █████
Bakerloo , Not sure Circle

While yes its true I cant recall the central one, with so few train lines and the length of the censorship making guesses quite easy, the redacted lengths should in my humble opinion either be edited, or just reverted.
Other than that, really nice idea, upvote from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Necropost but it's less of an issue in the UK as the whole population already gets healthcare

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Just generally +1.

Original idea, relatively plausable (I've never checked for a conductor when riding around the loop, I just assume they're there) and the fact that it doesn't work certain days gave me chills: the replacement is an actual person, just invisible. fuuuuuuuu

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This was going to be my guess. Despite the large number of Brits I've encountered on the site, us Yankees still seem to be the ones in control of The Foundation

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is kinda fun, but it bothers me that the Foundation couldn't come up with a better containment procedure than this. Here's an example "better" containment procedure:

One member of an MTF (posing as a police officer) always rides each train. This agent guards the driver compartment during shift changes, and if the scheduled driver does not arrive, drives the train himself.

Of course, I could see this procedure backfiring when the "ghost driver" attempts to take over, but it seems so obvious a thing to try (set up a situation where it is impossible for any train to ever lack a driver) that I'm surprised there is no incident log regarding an attempt to do so.

Perhaps more amusing would be if the assigned agent and the assigned driver always fail to show up on the same day… .

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Being very picky here, but where it lists the preferential tube lines, the blanked names are 8, 9 and 5 characters long respectively. There are no tube lines with a name of either 9 or 5 characters. The shortest tube line name is Circle, which is 6 characters. There are four tube lines which are 8 characters long, so that's fine. The 9 character blank could be extended to 10, which would indicate Piccadilly, or to 12 to indicate Metropolitan, or if you want to keep it so that you can't actually figure out precisely which lines are being indicated, there are also 2 lines with a 7 character name (Jubilee and Central).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love this one - it's great to have an SCP based in England. One minor issue, however - as a tube buff I noticed that the name "London Underground Metropolitan Railway" is incorrect. It should be "London Underground Railway" or even just "London Underground", as the third paragraph of the description implies that the phenomenon occurs on multiple Tube lines, and the Metropolitan Line is only one of them. Apart from that, great job!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is that blacked-out soccer team "Tottenham Hotspur" or something?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: *too distracted

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No sense losing your coal, eh?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't see why you have to leave in a chuff; it's no use railing against all these bad puns.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think it is time to switch tracks. Maybe a nice island vacation (Sodor, anyone?).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm so angry at this thread. I just need to go somewhere to blow off some steam.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: We'd better stop before we all go loco.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this entry.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Gentlemen, I think it's time this conversation reached the end of the line.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I can't believe you people engineered this train of thought.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like that the procedure is included in the description. Sometimes it can work, sometimes not. This is one of the times it does.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This really is a case of grievous misconductor.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I personally think the MTF should be the "Diesel Weasels". But that's just me.

Anyhoo nice SCP.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I see what you did there, don't think you can derail the thread with more puns.

wait what did I just sa… fffffffffffffuuuuuuu~!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Alternately "Association Football" because it also helps distinguish it from the Australian and Chinese versions. :) Anything involving feet and balls other than groin shots, I guess it's kind of inevitable.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There is always a degree of subjectivity with these things. Indeed there are a number of articles on the wiki that are rated quite highly, that I find dull or uninteresting. Should more people come here and raise similar issues as you have raised, then I will be happy to deal with them. Otherwise, I think I'll keep this as it is.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I am British. I chose to use the word soccer as it is universally understood, whereas football may be confused with the (inferior) American version.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wow. It is seriously just us two. Maybe we weren't trained right.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I liked that, but a British citizen would recognize it as 'football', rather than 'soccer'.
All in all, quite a good SCP

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
As apparently the only person to downvote this, I feel obligated to come here and explain why.

I don't have any problems with the execution, it's good and solid.

Concept however is extremely uninteresting to me. The whole replacement thing makes me think "Ok, so what?", and "Everyone goes crazy and gouges their eyes out" doesn't help at all, maybe even make it worse. Recently deleted SCP about groundhog couple that did absolutely nothing was more interesting to me than this.

This is not a suggestion to you to change something. +20 rating says that everything is quite nice as it is, and you can't please everyone. I just wanted to explain my downvote so you wouldn't wonder what caused it. I know I do when I see downvotes to my SCPs.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The effect will happen if at least one member of the train driving staff are not present at work due to illness or injury.

Wouldn't the Foundation make at least some effort to reduce the chance of the driving staff becoming sick or injured? Like providing them with better health care?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Also Football and birthdays.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: For some reason, the thing that makes this SCP for me is the fact that it takes union holidays. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I suppose it just depends on the train type. Also, how often would you think to check? Especially if you are a daily rider, why would you look to see if a driver is present for a train that is clearly being used? Secondly, that might be part of the oddity, that unless you are looking specifically, your eyes just glaze over and you don't think about it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
[whistling nonchalantly] Grammar mistakes?! I didn't see any grammar mistakes here! Such a basic error would be highly embarrassing for the original author! It's a good job there aren't any!

BTW if anyone else happens to see any clearly not there invisible grammar mistakes, then feel free to remove them.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Affected. AFFECTED. I can't vote on this right now because I was to distracted by the effects of you effecting your ineffective use of effected.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The reason I like the craziness in this one is that it is believable though. It's not "everyone on board goes crazy (TO DEAAATH)" but it is "everyone on board is under stress and reacts in different ways, we've noticed that some of these reactions are beyond what would be expected of natural causes"

That makes it feels like a more genuine haunting or fear effect beyond some cheezy thing where everyone always over reacts in the same terrible way. Things that make people lose control should always be random and inconsistent because people are different.

And imagine the setting… you're riding a train and it starts getting too fast, you panic and look around and everyone else is a little scared… and then it gets surreal, some people are holding their heads in pain and the nice old lady across the way starts tearing at her face and THEN and some big burly dudes are throwing gas grenades and trying to restrain her. That's a scene from a horror movie.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"affected trains", not "effected trains". And I like the idea, although personally I'd prefer if the passengers weren't affected in the second scenario other than the usual panic over "our train's not stopping and I think we're picking up speed!"

And honestly, I'm a little bit skeptical about that second scenario not happening often. Most trains I've been around, it's pretty visible whether there's a driver and most train stations have a mirror along the tracks for the driver to use to do a quick spot check as he's leaving the station. Maybe restrict it to people trying to interact with the driver by opening the door/curtain and attempts to interfere with the train?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Interesting, I actually like the craziness in this one for a change.

The only way this could be better is if the mysterious replacement is Gene Wilder as Willy Wonka. (Not really but still.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My Second SCP. I was trying to go for something that is extremely difficult to contain, due to it's extremely public location. As always, constructive criticism is welcome.
